The 'fleur-de-lis' refers to an ornamental design resembling a lily, which can be observed when there's an inward groove or indentation on certain teeth. This specific pattern often describes dens invaginatus where one tooth grows into another, creating this distinctive shape.

My answer: A

The "fleur-de-lis" description fits best with option A, dens invaginatus, as it accurately captures the characteristic inward growth that creates such a unique appearance in affected teeth.

The 'fleur-de-lis' feature indeed corresponds to the internal root resorption process seen in dens invaginatus (option A).
